text.title: ruby-rspec-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ruby-rspec-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Stubbing the method under test: agent stubs the very service method being spec'd — spec always passes regardless of the implementation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stubbing the SUT detaches the test from the real code path. Force prompts to explicitly name what is mocked vs real before generating the spec.

failure-mode.description: let shadowing: inner context redefines let(:user) with different attributes; outer spec examples silently use the wrong user.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: RSpec resolves let from the nearest enclosing scope, not the outermost. Lint via rubocop-rspec ContextLeakCheck to catch redefined lets.

failure-mode.description: before(:all) for DB setup: seed records created before the suite but within a transactional spec — transaction rolls back the seed, second run finds nothing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: before(:all) runs outside the per-example transaction. Use before(:each) or let! for records that must exist per example. Use shared_context for reusable setup.

failure-mode.description: build_stubbed with AR associations queried via count: user.posts.count issues a SQL query; build_stubbed does not persist to DB, so count returns 0 or errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: build_stubbed stubs AR persistence methods but not all query interfaces. For association assertions, allow(user).to receive(:posts).and_return([post]) or use create.

failure-mode.description: travel_to without the block form: time freezes and never resets; subsequent specs run with stale frozen time.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without travel_back or the block form, ActiveSupport::Testing::TimeHelpers leaves time pinned globally for the rest of the suite.

failure-mode.description: Factory cascade bloat: create(:order) triggers user → profile → avatar creation; one spec inserts 5 records, slowing the suite by an order of magnitude.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Factory traits and associations compose automatically; agents copy the nearest factory call. Use build_stubbed or define traits without auto-associations.

failure-mode.description: Shoulda-matchers on uncalled validators: validate_uniqueness_of(:email) passes even without a DB unique index, giving false confidence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Shoulda-matchers check model validations in memory; they do not probe the DB constraint. Pair with a request spec that hits the actual constraint.

failure-mode.description: VCR cassette drift: cassettes recorded against staging, replayed in CI, miss new fields; specs pass while integration is broken.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cassettes capture a snapshot of the response. When API contracts change, cassettes are stale but tests pass. Periodically re-record and review diffs.

failure-mode.description: Shared contexts hide behavior. A shared_context "as admin" mutates state in 8 ways; agent reading the spec sees only 1 line.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hidden setup makes debugging failures harder. Use shared examples for cross-cutting behavior assertions, shared contexts for reusable setup.

failure-mode.description: should-style matchers mixed with expect-style: codebases end up bilingual. RSpec disabled should for new specs but legacy lingers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inconsistent style makes it harder for agents to generate matching code. Enforce expect-style only.

failure-mode.description: Slow suite drift: naïve let! + before(:each) patterns rebuild the world per spec; suite hits 10+ min after a few hundred specs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Eager setup with let! creates DB writes per spec. Lazy let and build_stubbed keep suites under 1 min.

failure-mode.description: Mock-heavy specs that pass when refactored wrong. Agents stub everything; tests assert on stubs, not behavior. Mutation testing exposes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stubbing the SUT detaches tests from real code paths. Mock at boundaries, not internals. Verify real behavior not stubbed behavior.
